page: Amazon Web Services d3en.2xlarge is a dense storage instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores with hyper-threading) and a 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware characteristic is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 55,920 GB of local HDD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. The processor exhibits weak single-core performance in stress-ng and gzip compression tests, alongside a weak multi-core score in Geekbench. However, multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng, passmark CPU tests, and memory bandwidth (both read and write) fall within the average range. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also demonstrate average performance. The instance is not optimized for modern machine learning workloads, showing weak to poor performance in LLM inference tests for 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d3en.2xlarge offers a high density of local HDD storage relative to its compute and memory resources, making it a specialized option for storage-heavy workloads rather than compute-intensive tasks. The primary tradeoff is the reliance on spinning disk HDD storage rather than solid-state drives, which limits random I/O performance but maximizes raw capacity per resource unit. This hardware profile is best suited for workloads requiring massive sequential storage capacity, such as data warehousing, log processing, large-scale file systems, and backup repositories.
description: Amazon Web Services d3en.2xlarge is a dense storage instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a massive 55,920 GB of local HDD storage. Network performance is supported by a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though single-core execution and multi-core Geekbench scores are weak. Lacking a GPU, it is not suited for intensive machine learning tasks. The instance offers high storage density relative to compute resources, presenting a cost-efficient profile for capacity-driven workloads. It is best suited for data warehousing, log processing, and large-scale file systems where massive sequential storage capacity is required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.2xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 55,920 GB of local HDD storage, optimized for capacity-heavy workloads.

tagline: A dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for sequential data processing and archiving.

Massive 55,920 GB of local HDD storage capacity
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Weak single-core performance and limited LLM inference capabilities
